article_meta.title: Metro An open letter from the heart of Iran to America – Don’t give up on us By California Post Staff Published March 7, 2026, 7:11 p.m

raw_response: {"detections":[{"biasType":"Opinion Statements Presented as Fact","biasedText":"The Trump attack turned the page and changed everything in favor of the people, appropriately starting with the killing of the terrorist Ayatollah.","explanation":"The author's framing uses the word 'appropriately' to editorialize that the killing was fitting, and labels Khamenei a 'terrorist' as fact rather than as the source's characterization. While this is presented within the open letter format, the newspaper publishes it under its own editorial aegis without clear attribution or counterbalance."},{"biasType":"Bias by Omission","biasedText":"","explanation":"The article is presented entirely as a first-person account from one Iranian perspective, with no opposing viewpoints, no independent verification of claims (e.g., '30,000 killed in two days'), no humanitarian perspective on civilian casualties from the bombing, and no context about international criticism of the military strikes. The piece functions as advocacy rather than balanced reporting."},{"biasType":"Sensationalism and Emotionalism","biasedText":"Inside every one of those black body bags you saw after the January uprising was an innocent young person, with big dreams and a full heart.","explanation":"This emotionally charged language is presented in a news publication to evoke strong emotional reactions from readers, prioritizing emotional appeal over informational balance."},{"biasType":"Unsubstantiated claims","biasedText":"the regime killed more than 30,000 in just two days","explanation":"This is an extraordinary casualty claim repeated twice in the article without any sourcing, verification, or attribution to any reporting organization, human rights group, or independent body."},{"biasType":"Word Choice","biasedText":"so the Hitler of our century (Khamenei), his other allies may also reach their long-desired fate (martyrdom)","explanation":"While framed within the letter, the publication presents this inflammatory comparison of Khamenei to Hitler without any editorial note, contextual framing, or counterbalance, lending its platform to politically loaded rhetoric."},{"biasType":"Slant","biasedText":"Efforts have been made to protect his identity.","explanation":"The article is published as a news piece ('Metro' section, by 'California Post Staff') but is entirely a pro-bombing advocacy letter from a single perspective. The brief editorial note at the end does not disclose that this is an opinion/advocacy piece, structurally presenting one-sided advocacy as news reporting."}]}

detections.biasedText: The Trump attack turned the page and changed everything in favor of the people, appropriately starting with the killing of the terrorist Ayatollah.
detections.explanation: The author's framing uses the word 'appropriately' to editorialize that the killing was fitting, and labels Khamenei a 'terrorist' as fact rather than as the source's characterization. While this is presented within the open letter format, the newspaper publishes it under its own editorial aegis without clear attribution or counterbalance.

The article is presented entirely as a first-person account from one Iranian perspective, with no opposing viewpoints, no independent verification of claims (e.g., '30,000 killed in two days'), no humanitarian perspective on civilian casualties from the bombing, and no context about international criticism of the military strikes. The piece functions as advocacy rather than balanced reporting.

detections.biasedText: Inside every one of those black body bags you saw after the January uprising was an innocent young person, with big dreams and a full heart.
detections.explanation: This emotionally charged language is presented in a news publication to evoke strong emotional reactions from readers, prioritizing emotional appeal over informational balance.

detections.biasedText: the regime killed more than 30,000 in just two days
detections.explanation: This is an extraordinary casualty claim repeated twice in the article without any sourcing, verification, or attribution to any reporting organization, human rights group, or independent body.

detections.biasedText: so the Hitler of our century (Khamenei), his other allies may also reach their long-desired fate (martyrdom)
detections.explanation: While framed within the letter, the publication presents this inflammatory comparison of Khamenei to Hitler without any editorial note, contextual framing, or counterbalance, lending its platform to politically loaded rhetoric.

detections.biasedText: Efforts have been made to protect his identity.
detections.explanation: The article is published as a news piece ('Metro' section, by 'California Post Staff') but is entirely a pro-bombing advocacy letter from a single perspective. The brief editorial note at the end does not disclose that this is an opinion/advocacy piece, structurally presenting one-sided advocacy as news reporting.
